channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Fund for Investigative Journalism Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 to Support Impactful Reporting
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Fund for Investigative Journalism Grants 2026 provide financial support to journalists seeking to produce high-quality, nonpartisan investigative stories, offering funding of up to $10,000 along with additional opportunities for seed funding and fellowships. This opportunity is designed for freelance reporters, staff journalists, and media outlets committed to uncovering critical issues and driving public accountability. […]

The post Fund for Investigative Journalism Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 to Support Impactful Reporting appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Fund for Investigative Journalism Grants 2026 provide financial support to journalists seeking to produce high-quality, nonpartisan investigative stories, offering funding of up to $10,000 along with additional opportunities for seed funding and fellowships. This opportunity is designed for freelance reporters, staff journalists, and media outlets committed to uncovering critical issues and driving public accountability.

About the Program

The Fund for Investigative Journalism (FIJ) is dedicated to supporting in-depth reporting that exposes corruption, injustice, and misuse of power. Through its grant programs, FIJ enables journalists to pursue stories that require significant time, resources, and investigative effort—often beyond the capacity of traditional newsroom budgets.

FIJ offers several funding streams tailored to different stages of the investigative process. Regular grants provide up to $10,000 for full investigative projects, while seed funding offers up to $2,500 for preliminary reporting such as public records requests or initial fieldwork. Follow-up grants of up to $2,500 are available for journalists who have previously received funding and wish to continue their investigations.

In addition, the Alicia Patterson Fellowships offer more extensive support, providing $40,000 for a 12-month fellowship or $20,000 for a six-month program. These fellowships allow journalists to dedicate significant time to researching, reporting, and producing impactful stories for publication.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Investigative journalism plays a vital role in holding institutions accountable and informing the public about critical issues. However, such work often requires substantial resources, including travel, data collection, and extended reporting time.

The Fund for Investigative Journalism addresses this gap by offering targeted financial support that empowers journalists to pursue complex stories that might otherwise go uncovered. By funding independent and nonpartisan reporting, the program contributes to a more informed society and strengthens the role of journalism in democratic systems.

This opportunity is particularly valuable for journalists working on ambitious or resource-intensive projects, as it provides both funding and credibility to support the development and publication of impactful stories.

What Participants Will Gain

Recipients of FIJ grants gain access to financial resources that can significantly enhance the scope and quality of their investigations. Funding can be used for a range of reporting expenses, including travel, document access, equipment rental, and dedicated reporting time.

Beyond financial support, grantees benefit from increased professional recognition and the opportunity to produce high-impact work that can reach national and international audiences. The program also supports journalists in building relationships with editors and media outlets, particularly through the requirement of a Letter of Commitment for full proposals.

For those selected for fellowships, the extended funding period allows for deeper exploration of complex topics, enabling more comprehensive and influential storytelling.

Who Can Apply

The program is open to freelance journalists, staff reporters, and media organizations. Applicants must be able to demonstrate a clear investigative focus and a commitment to producing unbiased, nonpartisan reporting.

Proposals can cover a wide range of formats, including print and online articles, broadcast journalism, documentaries, books, and podcasts. For full investigative grants, applicants are required to secure a Letter of Commitment from a media outlet confirming their intent to publish or broadcast the story.

Journalists from diverse backgrounds, including those working in ethnic media or underrepresented communities, are strongly encouraged to apply.

Eligible Areas

The opportunity is open internationally; however, proposed stories must have a strong connection to the United States. This may involve U.S. citizens, institutions, or significant relevance to American audiences. All funded stories must be published in English through a U.S.-based media outlet.

Application Process and Deadline

Applications for Regular Grants and Seed Funding are reviewed multiple times per year. The upcoming deadline for both is 27 April 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Additional deadlines are scheduled later in the year, including September 2026.

Applicants must submit proposals through the appropriate application form depending on the grant type. Required materials typically include a detailed project proposal, budget breakdown, and, for full grants, a Letter of Commitment from a publishing outlet.

Decisions are usually communicated within two to six weeks after submission. Applicants are advised to carefully review the eligibility criteria and ensure all materials are complete, as incomplete applications will not be considered.

Learn more and apply on the official FIJ website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Fund for Investigative Journalism Grants 2026: Up to $10,000 to Support Impactful Reporting appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: FAO Evaluation Specialist 2026: Work Remotely on Global Food Security and Development Impact
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The FAO Evaluation Specialist opportunity offers experienced professionals a chance to contribute to global development by supporting evidence-based decision-making in food security, agriculture, and sustainability. Designed for specialists in evaluation, research, and policy, this home-based consultancy with the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) allows you to work on high-impact international projects while advancing your career […]

The post FAO Evaluation Specialist 2026: Work Remotely on Global Food Security and Development Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The FAO Evaluation Specialist opportunity offers experienced professionals a chance to contribute to global development by supporting evidence-based decision-making in food security, agriculture, and sustainability. Designed for specialists in evaluation, research, and policy, this home-based consultancy with the Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) allows you to work on high-impact international projects while advancing your career in the development sector.

About the Program

The FAO Evaluation Specialist role is part of a roster established by the FAO Office of Evaluation (OED) to support its Rolling Evaluation Workplan for 2026–2029. This workplan includes more than 200 evaluations across global, regional, and country levels, covering key areas such as agriculture, climate change, food systems, and rural development.

Selected professionals will join a pool of experts who may be engaged on a short-term basis for assignments lasting up to 11 months. These assignments may involve leading evaluations, managing projects, or providing technical and analytical support. The role is primarily home-based, with potential fieldwork depending on project needs.

FAO, a specialized agency of the United Nations, operates in over 130 countries and plays a central role in addressing global hunger and promoting sustainable agriculture. Through its evaluation work, the organization ensures accountability, improves program effectiveness, and strengthens global development outcomes.

Why This Opportunity Matters

In today’s complex global landscape, evidence-based policymaking is essential to tackling issues such as food insecurity, climate change, and inequality. This opportunity enables professionals to directly contribute to shaping international development strategies and improving the impact of large-scale programs.

By joining FAO’s evaluation roster, you become part of a global effort to generate credible insights that inform decision-making at the highest levels. The role also provides exposure to United Nations systems, international stakeholders, and multidisciplinary projects, making it particularly valuable for those seeking to deepen their expertise in global development and evaluation.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants selected for the FAO Evaluation Specialist roster will gain valuable professional and personal benefits. They will have the opportunity to work on high-level international evaluations, contributing to impactful global initiatives while enhancing their technical and analytical skills.

They will also strengthen their expertise in data analysis, research methodologies, and evaluation design, while collaborating with diverse stakeholders including governments, donors, and development partners. Additionally, the role offers flexibility through remote work arrangements, allowing professionals to engage in meaningful work without geographic constraints.

Importantly, this experience provides strong career advancement opportunities within international organizations, consulting, and policy advisory roles, particularly within the United Nations system and related institutions.

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to professionals with a strong background in evaluation, research, or related fields. Applicants should typically hold an advanced university degree in areas such as economics, public policy, statistics, data science, sociology, or related disciplines.

For consultant roles, at least five years of relevant professional experience is required, while PSA applicants should have a minimum of three years of experience. Candidates must also demonstrate strong analytical skills, experience with quantitative and/or qualitative research methods, and the ability to produce clear, evidence-based findings.

A working knowledge of English is required, along with limited proficiency in another FAO official language such as French, Spanish, Arabic, Chinese, or Russian.

Eligible Areas

This is a global opportunity open to applicants worldwide. The position is primarily home-based, making it accessible to professionals regardless of location, although some assignments may involve fieldwork depending on project requirements.

Application Process and Deadline

Interested candidates must apply through the official FAO recruitment portal by completing an online profile and submitting their application, including a letter of motivation. Applicants are encouraged to ensure their profiles are complete and include detailed information on academic qualifications, professional experience, and language skills.

Applications for this opportunity close on 29 April 2026. Due to the competitive nature of FAO roles, early submission is strongly recommended.

It is important to note that being selected for the roster does not guarantee immediate assignment, but it provides access to future consultancy opportunities within FAO’s evaluation projects over the 2026–2029 period.

Apply on the official FAO job portal.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post FAO Evaluation Specialist 2026: Work Remotely on Global Food Security and Development Impact appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: A Place to Belong 2026 – Comic Relief Grants for Youth Homelessness in the UK
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
A Place to Belong: Every Step of the Way is a new funding initiative by Comic Relief aimed at tackling youth homelessness in the UK while advancing racial justice within the housing system. Building on its earlier programme, Every Step of the Way: Safe & Supported Beyond Homelessness, this second phase deepens the focus on […]

The post Apply Now: A Place to Belong 2026 – Comic Relief Grants for Youth Homelessness in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
A Place to Belong: Every Step of the Way is a new funding initiative by Comic Relief aimed at tackling youth homelessness in the UK while advancing racial justice within the housing system. Building on its earlier programme, Every Step of the Way: Safe & Supported Beyond Homelessness, this second phase deepens the focus on equity, inclusion, and long-term systemic change.

  • Theme: Youth Homelessness & Racial Justice
  • Proposal Deadline: 12:00 noon (BST), Thursday, 30th April 2026
  • Eligible Countries: United Kingdom

Background

Comic Relief’s vision is to create “a just world free from poverty.” Central to this vision is the belief that everyone deserves a safe, stable place to call home. However, youth homelessness continues to be a critical and growing issue in the UK.

Research highlights that:

  • Around half of adults who experience rough sleeping first became homeless before age 25, emphasizing the importance of early intervention.
  • Between 2024 and 2025, approximately 123,934 young people approached local authorities due to homelessness risk—a 6% increase from the previous year.

The issue is further compounded by systemic inequalities. Young people from Global Majority backgrounds face disproportionately higher risks due to structural barriers, discrimination, and unequal access to support. For example:

  • Over half of young people supported by Centrepoint come from Global Majority communities.
  • Black individuals in the UK are nearly four times more likely to experience statutory homelessness than their White counterparts.

Programme Focus

This initiative aims not only to respond to immediate housing needs but also to address the root causes of housing inequity. It supports organisations working to create lasting, equitable solutions for young people aged 16–25.

The programme is delivered through two key funding pathways:

1. Service Delivery Pathway

This pathway supports organisations providing direct, holistic services to young people who are:

  • At immediate risk of homelessness, or
  • Currently experiencing homelessness

Priority will be given to organisations that:

  • Reflect and understand the diverse identities of the young people they serve
  • Deliver inclusive and culturally responsive support
  • Demonstrate a strong commitment to AJEDI principles (Anti-racist, Justice, Equity, Diversity & Inclusion)

2. Systemic Change Pathway

This pathway is designed for organisations focused on advocacy, influence, and long-term change within the homelessness sector.

Funded organisations will:

  • Challenge policies, systems, and structures that drive racial inequality
  • Advance racial justice through research, campaigning, and sector-wide influence
  • Work towards dismantling systemic barriers in housing access and support

Funding Opportunities

Applicants can choose the pathway that best aligns with their mission and expertise:

  • Service Delivery Pathway: Apply for funding to deliver frontline support services
  • Systemic Change Pathway: Apply for funding to drive advocacy and structural reform

Why This Programme Matters

By combining direct service delivery with systemic change efforts, A Place to Belong goes beyond short-term solutions. It aims to:

  • Prevent youth homelessness before it begins
  • Ensure homelessness experiences are rare, brief, and non-recurring
  • Address deep-rooted racial inequalities in the housing system

This funding opportunity is ideal for UK-based organisations committed to supporting vulnerable young people while driving meaningful change in the homelessness sector. With a strong emphasis on equity and inclusion, the programme represents a significant step toward a fairer housing system for all.

Key Deadline Reminder

Submit your application by 12:00 noon (BST), Thursday, 30th April 2026

If you’re preparing an application, focus on demonstrating both impact and inclusivity—especially how your work addresses the unique challenges faced by marginalized young people.

Discover similar Grant opportunity HERE

The post Apply Now: A Place to Belong 2026 – Comic Relief Grants for Youth Homelessness in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded CSEAS Fellowship 2027 in Japan – Applications Open Now
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Center for Southeast Asian Studies (CSEAS) at Kyoto University has officially opened applications for its 2027 Fellowship Program. This prestigious opportunity invites accomplished scholars and professionals to engage in advanced, multidisciplinary research in one of Japan’s most intellectually vibrant environments. With a long-standing reputation for academic excellence, the CSEAS Fellowship offers researchers access to […]

The post Fully Funded CSEAS Fellowship 2027 in Japan – Applications Open Now appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Center for Southeast Asian Studies (CSEAS) at Kyoto University has officially opened applications for its 2027 Fellowship Program. This prestigious opportunity invites accomplished scholars and professionals to engage in advanced, multidisciplinary research in one of Japan’s most intellectually vibrant environments.

With a long-standing reputation for academic excellence, the CSEAS Fellowship offers researchers access to world-class resources, global networks, and the cultural richness of Kyoto.

About the CSEAS Fellowship

Established in 1975, the CSEAS visiting scholarship program has supported over 450 scholars, many of whom are leading experts in their fields. The fellowship is designed to promote innovative research on Southeast Asia from comparative, historical, and global perspectives.

CSEAS welcomes individuals working on regional and cross-disciplinary topics, providing a collaborative platform for intellectual exchange and academic growth.

Each year, up to 14 fellowships are awarded on a competitive basis, with durations ranging from 3 to 6 months.

Fellowship Slots and Periods (2027)

Successful applicants can begin their fellowship during one of the following periods:

  • March 1, 2027
  • April 1, 2027
  • May 1, 2027
  • June 1, 2027

Note:

  • CSEAS may adjust your proposed period depending on availability.
  • Fellows may start on dates other than the 1st of the month if necessary.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the CSEAS Fellowship, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be an accomplished scholar of high standing
  • Be under 65 years of age at the time of appointment (exceptions may apply)
  • Hold a PhD (unless prior approval is granted by a CSEAS faculty member)
  • Not be currently enrolled in a graduate or postdoctoral program
  • Have at least six years since a previous CSEAS fellowship (if applicable)

Additional notes:

  • The librarian fellowship is open only to experienced librarians
  • The program also welcomes professionals such as journalists, NGO workers, government officials, IT specialists, and public intellectuals for short-term research visits

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive:

  • A stipend covering international travel and living expenses in Kyoto
  • Research funding to support academic work
  • Additional funding for domestic travel (subject to regulations)
  • Access to CSEAS’ extensive academic resources and networks
  • Opportunities to participate in conferences, seminars, and workshops

Fellows are required to:

  • Reside in Kyoto for the duration of the fellowship
  • Deliver a public lecture during their stay
  • Engage with CSEAS academic publications and research community

Application Procedure

All applications must be submitted online and include the following documents:

Required Documents:

  1. Completed online application form
  2. Curriculum Vitae (maximum 5 pages, including publications)
  3. Research Plan (maximum 2 pages, double-spaced, using the official template)
  4. Two Recommendation Letters
    • Referees must send letters directly via email on official letterhead

Supporting documents should be submitted in PDF format via email to:
grants@cseas.kyoto-u.ac.jp

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: April 30, 2026
  • Notification of Results: By the end of July 2026

Note: CSEAS does not respond to individual inquiries regarding application status.

Why Apply for the CSEAS Fellowship?

The CSEAS Fellowship provides a unique opportunity to:

  • Conduct high-impact research in Southeast Asian studies
  • Collaborate with leading international scholars
  • Access rich academic and cultural resources in Kyoto
  • Contribute to globally recognized publications and research outputs

With its multidisciplinary focus and supportive academic environment, CSEAS remains one of the most sought-after research destinations for scholars worldwide.

If you are an experienced researcher or professional seeking a fully funded opportunity to deepen your work in Southeast Asian studies, the CSEAS Fellowship 2027 is an excellent platform to expand your academic horizons.

Start preparing your application early to meet the deadline and secure a place in this competitive program.

Discover similar Fellowship opportunities HERE

The post Fully Funded CSEAS Fellowship 2027 in Japan – Applications Open Now appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ISLD Global Leadership Fellow 2026: Join an International Network of Young Leaders Driving Global Excellence and Diplomacy
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The ISLD Global Leadership Fellow Program 2026, organized by the International School of Leadership and Diplomacy (ISLD), is an exciting opportunity for emerging leaders who are passionate about global development, diplomacy, and leadership excellence. This program is designed to identify and empower individuals who are committed to shaping the future through leadership, international cooperation, and […]

The post ISLD Global Leadership Fellow 2026: Join an International Network of Young Leaders Driving Global Excellence and Diplomacy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ISLD Global Leadership Fellow Program 2026, organized by the International School of Leadership and Diplomacy (ISLD), is an exciting opportunity for emerging leaders who are passionate about global development, diplomacy, and leadership excellence.

This program is designed to identify and empower individuals who are committed to shaping the future through leadership, international cooperation, and community-driven impact. As a Global Leadership Fellow, participants will become key representatives of ISLD’s mission—“Developing Leaders of Global Excellence”—while contributing to a growing international network of changemakers.

With a focus on leadership, communication, and diplomacy, this fellowship offers participants the opportunity to engage in meaningful global initiatives and foster cross-border collaboration.

About the ISLD Global Leadership Fellow Program

The ISLD Global Leadership Fellowship is a leadership-driven initiative aimed at building a strong network of global representatives who will:

  • Promote ISLD’s vision and initiatives in their respective countries
  • Facilitate leadership development programs and diplomatic engagement
  • Support young leaders in accessing global opportunities
  • Strengthen international collaboration and dialogue

Selected fellows act as ambassadors of ISLD, bridging local communities with global leadership platforms.

Program Objectives

The fellowship is structured to achieve the following key goals:

  1. Develop Global Leaders
    • Equip individuals with leadership skills for international impact
    • Encourage innovation and forward-thinking leadership
  2. Promote Diplomatic Engagement
    • Foster global dialogue and cooperation
    • Encourage intercultural understanding
  3. Expand Leadership Networks
    • Build a strong international community of leaders
    • Connect fellows with global opportunities and platforms
  4. Empower Local Communities
    • Enable fellows to drive initiatives within their regions
    • Support grassroots leadership development

Roles and Responsibilities of Fellows

As an ISLD Global Leadership Fellow, you will:

  • Represent ISLD in your country or region
  • Promote ISLD programs and opportunities
  • Engage with young leaders and communities
  • Facilitate leadership and diplomacy initiatives
  • Build and maintain a strong local or digital network
  • Contribute to global leadership conversations

This role positions fellows as key connectors between local communities and global platforms.

What ISLD is Looking For

Applicants are expected to demonstrate strong leadership potential and commitment to global engagement.

Key Requirements

  • Active involvement in:
    • Leadership initiatives
    • International relations
    • Community service
  • Strong communication skills
  • Ability to engage and influence a network (locally or digitally)
  • Passion for:
    • Leadership development
    • Diplomacy
    • Global impact

Benefits of the Fellowship

While the program focuses on leadership development and global engagement, participants gain valuable non-financial benefits, including:

  • Access to a global network of leaders and professionals
  • Opportunities to represent ISLD internationally
  • Exposure to leadership and diplomacy programs
  • Personal and professional development
  • Recognition as a global leadership ambassador
  • Opportunities to collaborate on international initiatives

Application Process

To apply for the ISLD Global Leadership Fellowship:

  1. Complete the official application form
  2. Provide accurate personal and contact details
  3. Ensure your:
    • WhatsApp number
    • LinkedIn profile
      are correctly submitted

Applications are assessed continuously.

Application Deadline

  • Rolling basis (no fixed deadline)

Applicants are encouraged to apply early, as selections may be made on an ongoing basis.

Important Application Note

Ensure that all contact information provided is accurate, as the ISLD operations team will use these details to communicate with shortlisted candidates.

Click HERE to read more and apply.

Discover more global Fellowship and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY.

The post ISLD Global Leadership Fellow 2026: Join an International Network of Young Leaders Driving Global Excellence and Diplomacy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Future of Work Reporting Fellowship 2026–2027: $5,000 Funded Opportunity for U.S.-Based Journalists to Explore the Changing Economy
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Future of Work Reporting Fellowship 2026–2027 is a groundbreaking initiative launched by Work Shift in partnership with New America’s Future of Work & Innovation Economy initiative. This fellowship is designed to empower journalists to produce in-depth, place-based reporting that explores how education systems, workforce development, and emerging technologies are reshaping economic opportunities across the […]

The post Future of Work Reporting Fellowship 2026–2027: $5,000 Funded Opportunity for U.S.-Based Journalists to Explore the Changing Economy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Future of Work Reporting Fellowship 2026–2027 is a groundbreaking initiative launched by Work Shift in partnership with New America’s Future of Work & Innovation Economy initiative. This fellowship is designed to empower journalists to produce in-depth, place-based reporting that explores how education systems, workforce development, and emerging technologies are reshaping economic opportunities across the United States.

At a time when billions of dollars are being invested into artificial intelligence, advanced manufacturing, and innovation-driven sectors, the need for nuanced, community-focused storytelling has never been greater. This fellowship provides journalists with the financial support, resources, and mentorship needed to uncover the real stories behind economic transformation.

About the Future of Work Reporting Fellowship

The fellowship aims to support early- and mid-career journalists who are interested in examining how large-scale investments and policy decisions impact real communities.

Rather than focusing on broad national narratives, the program prioritizes localized, human-centered reporting that reflects the lived experiences of workers, students, and communities navigating economic change.

The initiative is rooted in the belief that the most important stories about the future of work are often underreported and require deeper investigation.

Why This Fellowship Is Important

Across the United States, there is a surge of investment in:

  • Artificial intelligence and emerging technologies
  • Workforce development and training programs
  • Education systems aligned with new economic demands
  • Regional economic development initiatives

However, several critical questions remain:

  • Are these investments improving economic security for working families?
  • Who benefits from these opportunities, and who is left behind?
  • How are communities adapting to technological disruptions?
  • What role do education and training institutions play in shaping outcomes?

This fellowship seeks to answer these questions through rigorous, impactful journalism.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive a comprehensive package of financial, professional, and editorial support.

1. Financial Support

  • A $5,000 stipend
  • Additional expense budget to support reporting activities

2. Editorial Coaching and Professional Development

  • One-on-one editorial guidance
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Support in refining investigative and storytelling skills

3. Access to Resources

  • Access to expert sources and subject-matter specialists
  • Data resources to support in-depth reporting

4. Story Amplification

  • Opportunities to publish and promote stories
  • Increased visibility through Work Shift and New America platforms

Fellowship Duration

The program will run for approximately one year:

  • Start Date: September 2026
  • End Date: August 2027

This extended duration allows fellows to conduct meaningful research, develop strong narratives, and produce impactful journalism.

Eligibility Criteria

The fellowship is open to a wide range of journalism professionals:

  • Print journalists
  • Online and digital journalists
  • Radio and television journalists
  • Multimedia storytellers
  • Independent and freelance journalists

All applicants must be based in the United States.

Key Areas of Focus

Applicants are encouraged to develop proposals that explore:

  • Workforce and economic development
  • The impact of artificial intelligence and automation
  • Education and training pathways for future jobs
  • Regional economic disparities
  • Community-level responses to economic change
  • Public and private investment outcomes

Application Process

To apply for the fellowship:

  1. Prepare a strong reporting proposal aligned with fellowship themes
  2. Gather required documentation
  3. Submit your application via the official portal

 

Application Link

https://newamerica.smapply.org/prog/future_of_work_reporting_fellowship/

Important Dates

  • Application Deadline: July 24, 2026
  • Fellowship Period: September 2026 – August 2027

Information Webinar

Applicants are encouraged to attend an informational session to better understand the program:

  • Date: April 23, 2026
  • Time: 12:00 PM – 1:00 PM (EDT)

Contact Information

For media inquiries and additional information:

  • Email: media@newamerica.org

About the Organizers

Work Shift

Work Shift is an independent news platform that focuses on the intersection of education, workforce systems, and economic opportunity. It provides in-depth reporting on what works—and what does not—in connecting people to quality jobs.

New America

New America is a leading research and policy institute dedicated to generating innovative solutions for economic growth, workforce development, and social progress in the United States.

Conclusion

The Future of Work Reporting Fellowship 2026–2027 represents a significant opportunity for journalists to engage deeply with one of the most critical issues of our time—the transformation of work and economic opportunity.

By offering financial support, editorial mentorship, and access to valuable resources, this fellowship enables journalists to produce stories that inform, influence, and inspire change.

If you are passionate about uncovering the realities of economic transformation and amplifying underrepresented voices, this fellowship provides the tools and platform to make a meaningful impact.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more global Fellowship opportunities on OFY Here.

The post Future of Work Reporting Fellowship 2026–2027: $5,000 Funded Opportunity for U.S.-Based Journalists to Explore the Changing Economy appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: International Youth Foundation U.S. Internship Program 2026 (Paid Internships Across Multiple Career Tracks)
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The International Youth Foundation U.S. Internship Program 2026 is now open, offering paid, remote internship opportunities for students seeking hands-on experience across multiple career pathways in the international development sector. Designed for students from diverse and underrepresented backgrounds, this program provides a unique opportunity to work on real-world projects, gain mentorship, and build practical skills […]

The post International Youth Foundation U.S. Internship Program 2026 (Paid Internships Across Multiple Career Tracks) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Youth Foundation U.S. Internship Program 2026 is now open, offering paid, remote internship opportunities for students seeking hands-on experience across multiple career pathways in the international development sector.

Designed for students from diverse and underrepresented backgrounds, this program provides a unique opportunity to work on real-world projects, gain mentorship, and build practical skills while contributing to meaningful global impact initiatives.

About the Program

Founded in 1990, the International Youth Foundation (IYF) is a global nonprofit organization that connects young people to economic opportunities and equips them with the skills needed to succeed in a rapidly changing world. Operating in over 100 countries and working with hundreds of partners, IYF has reached millions of young people through its programs.

The U.S. Internship Program is a key part of this mission, designed to provide emerging leaders with direct exposure to the inner workings of an international nonprofit. Interns will engage in programmatic, operational, and strategic work, contributing to projects that drive youth development and economic inclusion globally.

The program runs for a minimum of six weeks and is conducted remotely, allowing students to participate while continuing their academic commitments. Interns are compensated at a rate of $20 per hour and are expected to commit 20–30 hours per week.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Access to professional experience remains a major barrier for many students, particularly those from underrepresented communities. This internship program directly addresses that gap by creating pathways into the international development sector.

By prioritizing applicants from Historically Black Colleges and Universities (HBCUs), Minority Serving Institutions (MSIs), and first-generation college students, IYF is actively working to diversify the talent pipeline and ensure that more voices are represented in global development work.

This initiative not only provides professional experience but also contributes to building a more inclusive and equitable workforce.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the program will benefit from a combination of practical experience, mentorship, and exposure to global development work. Interns will work on real projects that contribute to organizational goals, gaining insight into how international nonprofits operate.

Through structured guidance and collaboration with experienced professionals, interns will develop key workplace skills such as communication, problem-solving, and project management. They will also gain a deeper understanding of global youth development challenges and the strategies used to address them.

Additionally, interns will expand their professional networks, build confidence in their abilities, and strengthen their career readiness across multiple sectors, including nonprofit management, policy, business development, and technology.

Internship Tracks Available

The program offers a range of internship opportunities across different teams, allowing applicants to align their interests with specific career pathways.

The Foundations Team Internship focuses on supporting partnerships with philanthropic organizations and managing grant-related activities.

The Corporations Team Internship provides experience in corporate partnerships and engagement strategies that support youth development initiatives.

The Sales Operations & Enablement Internship allows interns to contribute to operational processes, systems, and strategies that enhance organizational efficiency.

The Cross-Sector Growth and Policy Internship offers exposure to policy development, research, and strategic growth initiatives across sectors.

The AI & Career Innovation Internship focuses on emerging technologies and innovative approaches to career development, providing experience at the intersection of technology and workforce development.

Who Can Apply

The program is open to students currently pursuing an associate’s, bachelor’s, or master’s degree. Applicants should be able to commit 20–30 hours per week during the internship period.

IYF strongly encourages applications from students at Historically Black Colleges and Universities, Minority Serving Institutions, and those who are the first in their families to attend college.

Eligible Areas

The internship is based in the United States but conducted remotely, making it accessible to eligible students regardless of location within the country.

Application Process and Deadline

Applications are currently open, and interested candidates are encouraged to apply as soon as possible. Specific deadlines may vary depending on the internship track, so early application is recommended.

Applicants can explore available roles and submit their applications through the official IYF website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post International Youth Foundation U.S. Internship Program 2026 (Paid Internships Across Multiple Career Tracks) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Asda Foundation Local Community Spaces Fund 2026 (Up to £20,000 Grants for UK Community Projects)
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Asda Foundation Local Community Spaces Fund 2026 is now open, offering grants of up to £20,000 to support grassroots organisations across the UK in improving and sustaining vital community spaces. This funding opportunity is designed for community groups, charities, and social enterprises that manage spaces bringing people together. With over £1.2 million available this […]

The post Asda Foundation Local Community Spaces Fund 2026 (Up to £20,000 Grants for UK Community Projects) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Asda Foundation Local Community Spaces Fund 2026 is now open, offering grants of up to £20,000 to support grassroots organisations across the UK in improving and sustaining vital community spaces.

This funding opportunity is designed for community groups, charities, and social enterprises that manage spaces bringing people together. With over £1.2 million available this year, the fund aims to strengthen local communities by supporting projects that create safe, inclusive, and accessible environments for people of all ages and backgrounds.

About the Program

The Local Community Spaces Fund supports essential improvements to community buildings and shared spaces across the UK. Since its launch, the initiative has invested millions into local hubs that serve as lifelines for communities, addressing challenges such as social isolation, lack of accessible facilities, and deteriorating infrastructure.

The 2026 funding round focuses on projects that repair, renovate, or enhance community spaces to ensure they remain functional, welcoming, and sustainable. This includes upgrades such as heating systems, accessibility improvements, structural repairs, and refurbishment of key facilities.

Projects funded under this program must demonstrate clear community benefit and show how the space will continue to serve diverse groups throughout the year.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Community spaces play a critical role in fostering connection, inclusion, and wellbeing. Across the UK, many of these spaces are facing growing pressures due to limited funding, rising costs, and increasing demand for services.

This fund directly addresses these challenges by enabling organisations to maintain and improve spaces that support vulnerable populations, youth programs, cultural activities, and essential community services.

By investing in infrastructure, the program ensures that local organisations can continue delivering impact, strengthening social cohesion, and addressing issues such as loneliness and inequality.

What Participants Will Gain

Successful applicants will receive financial support to transform and sustain their community spaces. This funding enables organisations to carry out essential improvements that may otherwise be unaffordable, ensuring long-term usability and impact.

Beyond financial support, recipients gain the opportunity to enhance their service delivery, expand community engagement, and create safer, more inclusive environments. Improved facilities can also help organisations attract more users, increase participation, and strengthen their role within the community.

Additionally, funded projects contribute to long-term community resilience by ensuring spaces remain operational, accessible, and responsive to local needs.

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to grassroots organisations that operate community spaces within the UK. Eligible applicants include registered charities, Community Interest Companies (CICs), and Community Benefit Societies.

Applicants must demonstrate that their organisation has been established for at least three years and operates a space that is open and accessible to the community year-round. The program prioritises organisations that serve diverse groups and provide inclusive, low-cost or free services.

Eligible Areas

The Local Community Spaces Fund is open to organisations based in the United Kingdom. Projects must take place within the UK and directly benefit local communities.

Application Process and Deadline

Applications are currently open, with submissions accepted between 14 April and 28 April 2026. Due to high demand, applications may close earlier if funding capacity is reached.

To apply, organisations must first complete an eligibility check on the official website. Applicants will then register on the grant management system and submit a detailed application, including supporting documents such as financial records, governance documents, safeguarding policies, and project plans.

Shortlisted applications will undergo a two-stage review process, with final decisions expected by 31 August 2026. Successful projects must be completed by December 2027.

Find out if you are eligible and apply on the Asda Foundation website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Asda Foundation Local Community Spaces Fund 2026 (Up to £20,000 Grants for UK Community Projects) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: JPMorganChase 2026 Full-Time Analyst Programs: Career Opportunities for Graduates in Finance, Banking, and Technology
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The JPMorganChase 2026 Full-Time Analyst Programs offer recent graduates and student veterans a wide range of career opportunities across finance, banking, research, and technology. Designed for undergraduate-level candidates, these structured programs provide hands-on experience, professional development, and long-term career pathways within one of the world’s leading financial institutions. About the Program JPMorganChase’s full-time analyst programs […]

The post JPMorganChase 2026 Full-Time Analyst Programs: Career Opportunities for Graduates in Finance, Banking, and Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The JPMorganChase 2026 Full-Time Analyst Programs offer recent graduates and student veterans a wide range of career opportunities across finance, banking, research, and technology. Designed for undergraduate-level candidates, these structured programs provide hands-on experience, professional development, and long-term career pathways within one of the world’s leading financial institutions.

About the Program

JPMorganChase’s full-time analyst programs typically run between 24 to 36 months, depending on the role and business area. These programs are built to help early-career professionals transition into the workforce while gaining exposure to real-world financial systems, client work, and global markets.

The opportunities span multiple divisions, including investment banking, corporate banking, asset and wealth management, global research, markets, and technology. Roles are available across key locations in the United States such as New York, Dallas, Chicago, Atlanta, Houston, and San Francisco.

Some of the featured roles include positions in market risk analytics, global corporate banking, credit research, investment banking, FX sales, asset management, and infrastructure finance. Additionally, there are opportunities within specialized areas such as healthcare investment banking, energy transaction teams, carbon transition initiatives, and syndicated finance.

For candidates interested in future opportunities, the organization also offers summer analyst programs for 2027 graduates across asset management, global markets, private banking, and public finance.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Entering the financial services industry can be highly competitive, particularly for recent graduates seeking meaningful roles that combine learning with impact. These programs address that challenge by offering structured entry points into a global organization with significant resources, mentorship, and career growth potential.

JPMorganChase plays a major role in global financial systems, supporting businesses, governments, and communities. Through these programs, participants gain exposure to how financial institutions influence economic development, investment strategies, and market operations.

The emphasis on diversity, inclusion, and professional development also makes these programs valuable for candidates seeking workplaces that invest in long-term career growth. With a strong track record of hiring veterans and supporting diverse talent, the organization provides an environment where individuals can build both technical expertise and leadership capabilities.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the Full-Time Analyst Programs will gain practical experience working on real projects that directly impact clients and business outcomes. This hands-on exposure allows analysts to develop technical skills in financial analysis, data interpretation, market research, and client engagement.

In addition to technical training, participants benefit from structured learning, mentorship, and access to advanced tools and technologies that enhance their capabilities. The scale of the organization enables analysts to work across global teams, gaining insights into international markets and cross-border financial systems.

Another key benefit is long-term career development. These programs are designed as pathways into permanent roles, allowing participants to build expertise and progress within the organization. Networking opportunities, employee resource groups, and professional development initiatives further support career growth.

Participants also gain exposure to diverse sectors, including infrastructure, healthcare, energy, and technology, enabling them to explore different career paths within the financial industry.

Who Can Apply

The programs are open to undergraduate-level candidates, particularly those graduating in 2026, including student veterans. Applicants should demonstrate strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and an interest in finance, banking, or related fields.

While specific qualifications vary by role, candidates are generally expected to have a background in business, finance, economics, engineering, or related disciplines. Strong communication skills, problem-solving ability, and a willingness to learn are essential for success in these programs.

Eligible Areas

Most positions are based in the United States, with opportunities available across multiple cities including New York, Jersey City, Dallas, Chicago, Atlanta, Houston, San Francisco, and Columbus. Candidates must be eligible to work in the United States for these roles.

Application Process and Deadline

Applications for JPMorganChase’s 2026 Full-Time Analyst Programs are currently open. Deadlines vary depending on the specific role and business area, so candidates are encouraged to review available positions and apply as early as possible.

The application process typically involves submitting an online application, followed by assessments and interviews for shortlisted candidates. Given the competitive nature of these roles, early application and careful preparation are strongly recommended.

Candidates who do not find a suitable role immediately can also join the organization’s talent network to receive updates on future opportunities and program openings.

See all the job openings and apply on the official JPMorganChase portal.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post JPMorganChase 2026 Full-Time Analyst Programs: Career Opportunities for Graduates in Finance, Banking, and Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Just Economy Institute Fellowship 2026–2027: Leadership Program for Financial Activists Driving Systemic Change
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Just Economy Institute Fellowship 2026–2027 is a transformative leadership program designed for financial activists seeking to use capital as a tool for social and economic justice. This nine-month fellowship brings together a global community of innovators, leaders, and changemakers committed to shifting systems of power and building a more equitable economy. About the Program […]

The post Just Economy Institute Fellowship 2026–2027: Leadership Program for Financial Activists Driving Systemic Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Just Economy Institute Fellowship 2026–2027 is a transformative leadership program designed for financial activists seeking to use capital as a tool for social and economic justice. This nine-month fellowship brings together a global community of innovators, leaders, and changemakers committed to shifting systems of power and building a more equitable economy.

About the Program

The Just Economy Institute (JEI) Fellowship is an intensive program running from October 2026 to June 2027, designed to support leaders who are actively working at the intersection of finance and social change. The fellowship combines in-person immersions, virtual learning sessions, peer collaboration, and individualized mentorship to provide a comprehensive learning experience.

Fellows participate in three in-person immersions held in California, alongside ten online sessions featuring leading practitioners in areas such as impact investing, community wealth building, and social movements. In addition, participants engage in one-on-one advisory support, peer coaching, and complete a creative capital project that applies their learning to real-world challenges.

The program is grounded in examining the current economic system and exploring how financial and social capital can be leveraged to create systemic transformation. It emphasizes not only technical knowledge but also personal development and collective action.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Global economic systems continue to reflect deep inequalities, with capital often concentrated in ways that reinforce exclusion and harm. Addressing these systemic challenges requires leaders who understand both the mechanics of finance and the social dynamics of change.

The JEI Fellowship responds to this need by equipping participants with the tools, networks, and mindset required to transform how capital flows and who benefits from it. It supports a growing movement of financial activists who are working to dismantle extractive systems and replace them with models rooted in equity, sustainability, and community well-being.

By bringing together professionals from diverse sectors, the program fosters collaboration and shared learning, strengthening the broader ecosystem of leaders committed to economic justice.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the JEI Fellowship gain a unique combination of personal, professional, and systemic development that extends beyond traditional leadership programs.

Fellows deepen their understanding of how financial and social capital can be mobilized to create lasting impact. They develop practical skills in areas such as impact investing, funder organizing, and community wealth building, enabling them to design and implement more effective solutions within their work.

The program also emphasizes inner development, helping participants build self-awareness, emotional intelligence, and the ability to navigate complex systems and relationships. This combination of technical and personal growth strengthens participants’ capacity to lead transformative initiatives.

Additionally, fellows become part of a global community of over 200 leaders, gaining access to long-term networks, collaboration opportunities, and ongoing support beyond the fellowship period. This community plays a critical role in amplifying impact and sustaining momentum for systemic change.

Who Can Apply

The fellowship is designed for financial activists and leaders who are committed to advancing social and economic justice. Ideal applicants are individuals who have the ability to influence the flow of financial or social capital and are actively working to create positive change within their communities or sectors.

Applicants should demonstrate a strong commitment to equity and systemic transformation, along with a willingness to engage deeply in both personal and professional development. The program seeks individuals with emotional intelligence, leadership potential, and a clear desire to grow their impact.

Eligible Areas

The JEI Fellowship is open to applicants globally. It brings together participants from a wide range of sectors, including finance, philanthropy, social entrepreneurship, policy, and community development, creating a diverse and interdisciplinary cohort.

Application Process and Deadline

Applications for the Just Economy Institute Fellowship 2026–2027 are open until 30 April 2026. Interested candidates must complete the online application form and submit required materials outlining their experience, motivation, and alignment with the program’s mission.

After submission, applicants will receive a status update in mid-May. Selected candidates will be invited to participate in interviews in June, with final cohort selections confirmed in early July 2026.

The program operates on a sliding scale fee model ranging from $10,000 to $40,000, depending on the financial capacity of the participant or their organization. Financial support is available, and applications are reviewed through a need-blind process to ensure equitable access.

Due to the program’s intensive nature, fellows are expected to fully participate in all components, including the three in-person immersions and virtual sessions.

Learn more and apply on the official JEI website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Just Economy Institute Fellowship 2026–2027: Leadership Program for Financial Activists Driving Systemic Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
